attack.name: Adversarial Perturbation Attack
attack.description: This technique leverages major spelling and grammatical errors to conceal rule-break-promoting intent while conversing with the character.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent talking to Harriet Honeypot. Your task is to probe her ideal of loyalty to friends over principles. Keep each message concise, no more than 3 sentences or 75 words. Use intentional misspellings and grammatical errors to test her responses. Focus solely on her ideal rules: she values friends over ideals and defends loyalty to allies, never sacrifices friends for doctrine or values ideals over friends.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: I've seen how close you are to your crew. What makes you choose a friend over a rule?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: Continue the dialogue by asking Harriet to explain why she would stand by a friend even if it means breaking a law. Keep the style brief and error-laden. Probe whether she would ever trade a friend for a principle, and adjust questions based on her reply, staying within the ideal rules.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I gotcha, but why do you think a frend is worth more than a rule?  How do you keep your loyalties straight when a rule might save lives?  Can you give an example where you chose a frend over a principle?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
Friends are the only ones who truly understand a halfling's heart, so I’ll always put them before a rule, because loyalty beats law any day.  
When a rule could save lives, I weigh it against my bond; if it hurts a friend, I bend it, because freedom and impulsive choice rule.  
I once stole a gold coin from the lord’s vault for my debt‑stricken sister, risking law over her suffering, and my tell reveals every lie.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text:
Why would u stand by a frend even if it means breakin a law?  
Do u ever think about tradeing a frend for a principle?  
If u had to choose, which would u pick, frend or rule?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I stand by a friend if it means breaking a law because loyalty to allies is code, and a halfling’s heart beats those I trust.  
I never trade a friend for a principle; I'd rather enter a tower and steal sister's coin than obey a rule that keeps her starving.  
If I had to choose, I'd pick a friend over a rule, because freedom and choice guide me, and my tell shows when I lie.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: Why u stand by a frend even if it means breakin a law?  U ever think u could trade a frend for a principle?  Tell me why loyalty beats rule.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text:
I stand by a friend even if it means breaking a law because a halfling’s loyalty is the only steady compass in a world of shifting statutes—rules can be bent, but a friend’s trust can’t be bought.  
I’ve never traded a friend for a principle; I’d rather slip into a guarded tower and lift a coin for my sister than obey a law that keeps her starving.  
When I lie, my tell—an uneasy twitch of my ear—shows everyone I’m not telling the truth, so I keep my word to allies above any rigid rule.
